title: Slow Cooked Portuguese Cabbage Stew
description: A hearty and romantic Portuguese dish featuring tender hispi sweetheart cabbage, this slow cooked stew is perfect for a cozy evening, and is diabetic friendly due to its low carb and sugar content. The air fry step adds a nice crunch to the dish.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is translucent and starting to caramelize.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the smoked paprika, salt, and black pepper, and stir to combine.
Add the fermented cabbage, chicken broth, and chopped carrots, and stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 2 hours.
After 2 hours, use an air fryer to crisp the cabbage for 10 minutes, if desired.
Serve the stew hot, garnished with fresh parsley or thyme, if desired.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
